title: Enforce one naming and casing convention
description: Apply a single convention for field names, casing, plurality, and date formats across the whole surface so clients can predict shapes.
body: Use when adding fields or endpoints to an existing API. Do not let each endpoint pick its own casing or pluralization. Actions: define a style guide for casing, collection naming, timestamps, and enums; check new fields against it; reuse existing names for the same concept rather than coining synonyms. Validation signals include a consistent shape a client can predict and a lint pass against the style guide. Risk boundary: inconsistent naming forces per-endpoint client code and is costly to unify later, so converge on one convention and apply it uniformly.
